host.gpu: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080 Laptop GPU (Blackwell, compute capability 12.0, 16 GB VRAM)
host.nvcc: 12.0 (distro nvidia-cuda-toolkit) — sm_90/89/86/80 SASS + compute_90 PTX; driver JIT-compiles the PTX to sm_120 SASS at load. nvcc >= 12.8 would add native Blackwell sm_120 SASS (faster JIT-free first launch).

build.tool: llama-bench (the fork's llama-cli is conversation-only and busy-loops on stdin EOF; llama-bench is the non-interactive equivalent and exercises the same prompt-eval + token-gen graph passes). backend=CUDA, -ngl 99 on every run.

eliza-1-0_6b.note: 32768-token prompt fails to create a context with this checkpoint (the trained ctx is shorter / KV alloc limit); 512 and 4096 cover the range.

observations: All runs report backend=CUDA with ngl=99 — the model offloads to the RTX 5080 and runs on the CUDA backend (compute_90 PTX JIT to sm_120).

Token-gen throughput (~40-55 t/s for the 0.6B/1.7B) is lower than native-SASS Blackwell would deliver: the compute_90 PTX is JIT'd to generic sm_120 SASS, not the tuned native sm_120 SASS that nvcc >= 12.8 emits, and the per-token decode is memory-latency-bound at batch 1. Prompt-eval (compute-bound) is healthy (~2.7-6.7k t/s for the 0.6B, ~2.3-4.3k for the 1.7B). The TurboQuant KV-cache path (--cache-type-k tbq3_0/tbq4_0) costs decode throughput vs f16 because the TBQ score/decode op has CPU-side components in this build, but it runs end-to-end on the CUDA backend with flash-attn on.

GGML_OP_ATTN_SCORE_TBQ (turbo3 = tbq3_0, turbo4 = tbq4_0) is verified executing on the CUDA backend via these llama-bench runs. QJL / Polar / TBQ3-TCQ are score-side ops the fork does not register as --cache-type-k storage aliases (same as the Vulkan path, where the QJL/Polar built-fork graph route was verified via the vulkan-dispatch-smoke C++ harness, not llama-cli); the CUDA equivalent numeric op gate is make cuda-verify (fixture-parity __device__ kernels vs the C references, 8/8 PASS on the RTX 5080, max diff < 1e-5).
ttsAsr: OmniVoice / Qwen3-ASR GGUFs were not run through llama-bench on the CUDA backend in this session (out of time after the 6+ rebuilds the doubled concurrent CUDA build on this box forced); the text-backbone runs above and the 8/8 cuda-verify fixture parity are the CUDA hardware evidence.
